questions.question: Why is it wrong to make HTTP calls or access @Input properties in an Angular component's constructor?
options: HTTP calls are blocked during constructor execution for security reasons
@Input properties are not yet set and injected services may not be fully initialized
The constructor runs in a web worker and cannot access the DOM or HTTP
Angular prohibits any method calls inside constructors

questions.question: What is the Spring Boot equivalent of Angular's ngOnInit?
options: @Autowired
@Component
@PostConstruct
@ServiceInit

questions.question: An Angular component subscribes to an interval observable in ngOnInit but never unsubscribes. What is the consequence?
options: Angular automatically unsubscribes when the component's route changes
The interval keeps running and holds a reference to the component after it's destroyed — a memory leak
The subscription is garbage collected when the component's template is removed from the DOM
The Observable throws a SubscriptionClosedError when the component is destroyed

questions.question: In what order does Angular call lifecycle hooks on a component's first render?
options: ngOnInit → ngOnChanges → constructor
constructor → ngOnInit → ngOnChanges
constructor → ngOnChanges → ngOnInit
ngOnChanges → constructor → ngOnInit

questions.question: What does takeUntilDestroyed(destroyRef) do in Angular 16+?
options: It converts a Promise to an Observable that completes on destroy
It automatically unsubscribes from an Observable when the component is destroyed
It prevents the component from being destroyed while a subscription is active
It retries the Observable if it errors before the component is destroyed
